topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The music swelled, a saccharine wave washing over the crowded ballroom. I clutched my champagne flute so tightly my knuckles were white, the crystal digging into my palm. Then, across the glittering expanse of dancing guests, I saw him. Liam. He was laughing, his arm draped casually around a woman whose smile seemed effortless, a light I wasn't allowed to touch. My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow intake that felt like swallowing shards of glass. Every muscle in my body tightened, a cold knot forming just beneath my sternum. I forced a smile, a brittle, unnatural thing that felt like cracking plaster. I needed air, needed distance. I turned abruptly, my heels clicking a frantic rhythm against the polished floor, desperate to melt into the periphery of the room. The air suddenly felt too thick, too heavy to draw into my lungs.

[story 2]

It was a cruel twist of fate, or perhaps just bad timing, that brought me face-to-face with Alex at Sarah’s wedding. He was standing near the buffet, looking impossibly composed, a picture of easy contentment. My stomach performed a violent flip, a sickening lurch that made me want to sink right through the marble floor. I watched him for a beat too long, cataloging the way the light caught his hair, the familiar set of his jaw. A wave of nausea washed over me, so intense it made my vision swim. I mumbled an excuse to a passing waiter and pivoted, my retreat jerky and uncoordinated. My hands were clammy, slick with a sweat that had nothing to do with the warm room. I needed to disappear, to become invisible, because the proximity of him felt like a physical assault on my fragile composure.

[story 3]

The noise of the celebration was a relentless barrage, grating against my nerves like sandpaper. I was navigating the throng of well-wishers, trying to maintain a semblance of normalcy, when I spotted him near the entrance. Liam. Seeing him triggered an immediate, visceral tightening in my chest, a constriction that stole my ability to breathe deeply. My focus narrowed instantly; the rest of the room dissolved into a blurry wash of color. I felt a distinct urge to flee, a primal need to escape the magnetic pull of his presence. I kept my gaze fixed on a distant architectural detail, my jaw clenched so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. Every instinct screamed at me to turn around, to walk into the nearest exit and vanish, leaving this suffocating atmosphere behind.
